questions.text: When you're shopping for everyday items — tech gear, household stuff, or things for your space — do you research thoroughly before buying, or do you tend to just get what feels right in the moment?

When you're playing video games — whether it's a quick session or a deep dive — do you tend to go for short, pick-up-and-play games, or do you prefer ones where you can sink hours into progress and story?

When you're planning a vacation or trip — how far out do you typically start thinking about it? Do you like having something booked months in advance, or do you prefer keeping things flexible and deciding closer to the date?

What's your approach to maintaining important documents and records — things like passports, insurance policies, financial accounts, and IDs? Do you keep digital copies synced somewhere, physical originals organized in a specific place, or have some other system?

When you think about your daily environment — the sounds, visual clutter, and overall sensory input around you — do you prefer things quiet and minimal, or do you actually work better with some background noise and visual activity happening?
